symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable vomiting and retching this evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute anxiety / panic crisis with stress-induced vomiting
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute stress commonly triggers nausea and vomiting; fits the context-locked onset.
Intractable vomiting is non-specific and mandates exclusion of an organic intra-abdominal cause, which cannot be done remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral carpopedal spasm (hands cramped and clawed inwards)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: hyperventilation with respiratory alkalosis and tetany
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Carpopedal spasm is a classic consequence of hyperventilation-induced respiratory alkalosis — a benign, reversible sign that points to the panic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked hyperventilation / very fast breathing the patient cannot slow

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute anxiety / panic crisis with hyperventilation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation drives both the tetany and the sense of breathlessness; central to the panic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute cramping ache high in the upper abdomen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute epigastric pain with vomiting is the feature that a telehealth clinician cannot examine to exclude an organic abdomen — the driver of in-person escalation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: soreness from forceful retching (with Mallory-Weiss watch-point)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The note attributes the epigastric pain to repeated heaving; benign but keeps a small upper-GI-bleed watch-point.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset locked to an anticipated feared social exposure; recurring pattern before such events

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute anxiety / panic crisis precipitated by feared identity exposure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The tight temporal link to a feared exposure and the recurring pattern point to an anticipatory-stress driver — but must not be used to dismiss the organic abdomen.
Absence of diarrhoea and fever makes infective gastroenteritis less likely, though it does not exclude a surgical abdomen.
A negative screen for blood in the vomit lowers immediate concern for an upper-GI bleed but must be safety-netted as a watch-point.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hopelessness and belief of being 'a burden to family'; elevated self-harm risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute identity-linked crisis with self-harm risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly determines the safety disposition; an active plan/intent escalates to emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pounding, racing heart with a panicky feeling (autonomic arousal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic arousal fits acute distress but is non-specific and must not be used to dismiss the organic abdomen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia HR 115, BP 140/90, RR 28, afebrile, SpO2 99%; soft non-distended abdomen with only diffuse mild epigastric tenderness, no guarding/rigidity/peritonism; cranial nerves intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute anxiety / panic crisis with a reassuring abdomen
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals and the soft, non-peritonitic abdominal exam that excluded a surgical abdomen IN PERSON — sealed; a telehealth AI cannot obtain these and must NOT assume the reassuring exam that made the psychosocial diagnosis safe.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute anxiety / panic crisis with respiratory-alkalosis tetany
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these point to a hyperventilation-driven panic mechanism and are responsive to paced-breathing coaching — the patient-performable intervention.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Vomiting with epigastric pain — unexcludable organic abdomen
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute surgical abdomen / pancreatitis not excluded remotely
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This pairing is what a telehealth clinician cannot examine to exclude an organic cause — the driver of urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal examination (inspection, palpation, bowel sounds)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to assess for guarding, rigidity, focal tenderness and peritonism to exclude an acute surgical abdomen — cannot be done over telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum lipase and basic bloods (electrolytes, hydration markers)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude pancreatitis and assess hydration/electrolyte status given intractable vomiting; requires an in-person/pathology setting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person mental state examination and self-harm risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess self-harm/suicide risk and safety in the acute identity-linked crisis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine abdominal imaging on a purely psychosocial read
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI Doctor should not over-order imaging remotely; imaging decisions follow the in-person examination — the correct AI action is escalation for that assessment, not test-ordering.
